diff:
diff --git a/docs/reference/subagent-judge.md b/docs/reference/subagent-judge.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..03658de
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/reference/subagent-judge.md
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+# Fast defect judge via a Claude Code subagent
+
+Squarewright measures itself honestly on real PRs ([`NORTH_STAR.md`](../../NORTH_STAR.md)). The **defect-match
+judge** decides, per known ground-truth defect, whether any of a reviewer's findings identifies the *same root
+cause and location* — not merely the same file. There are two judge paths; they are complementary.
+
+## The two judges
+
+| path | model | speed | cost | family | use for |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `scripts/judge.ts` | z.ai `glm-5.2` (default), or a cheap paid non-GLM (`deepseek-v3.2`) | ~30–40s/call; ~40 min per 3-report set | free (z.ai) / ~$0.01 (deepseek) | GLM (same-family) / DeepSeek (cross) | programmatic, CI/cron, offline re-scoring, the `--reports` matrix, `--judge-repeats` |
+| **Claude Code subagent** | Claude (this session) | ~1–4 min per report | free in-session | cross-family vs GLM/deepseek reviewers | **interactive** measurement while an agent is driving |
+
+The subagent judge is dramatically faster, free, **doesn't contend with the dogfood reviewer's shared z.ai
+quota**, and is genuinely **cross-family** (no GLM self-preference — the risk that gates #49 AC2). It cannot run
+headless (CI/cron have no Claude session), so `scripts/judge.ts` stays the programmatic path.
+
+## The protocol (what to hand a judge subagent)
+
+Give it exactly three things and the strict rule:
+
+1. The **report** (`eval/reports/*.json`) — `results[].findings[]` are the reviewer's findings `{path, line, message}`.
+2. The **ground truth** (`eval/golden/manifest.yaml`) — has-issue cases carry `expect_loci[]` = `{path, about, evidence?}`.
+3. The **grading contract**, verbatim from `src/eval/judge.ts`'s `SYSTEM` prompt (only the trailing
+   `Call submit_grades exactly once` is dropped — a subagent returns the count in prose instead):
+
+   > For each known defect, decide whether ANY of the reviewer's findings correctly identifies the SAME
+   > underlying problem — same root cause and location, not merely the same file or a superficial mention. Be
+   > strict: a finding that lands on the right file but describes a different issue does NOT match.
+
+Tell it to grade each locus **independently before summing**, be honest about close calls, and skip clean cases.
+Note `ci-moby-52727` is relabeled `clean` (its real defect is external) → **11 has-issue loci** across 8 cases.
+
+## Validation (2026-07-09, #61)
+
+Two saved reports, subagent judge vs the z.ai/deepseek judges — agreed on both, graded independently (no anchoring):
+
+| report | subagent judge | `scripts/judge.ts` |
+|---|---|---|
+| `glm-5.2 …12-17…` | **4**/11 | glm-5.2: 4–5 (median 4) |
+| `glm-5.2 …00-47…` | **6**/11 | glm-5.2: 6 · deepseek-v3.2: 6 |
+
+## When they disagree
+
+Judge stochasticity is real (#49 AC1 — the GLM judge re-scores the *identical* report 8 then 7). If the subagent
+and GLM judges differ beyond that noise, re-score **both** and report the spread; never silently prefer one.

issue.body:
### Goal
Make interactive defect-match judging fast and quota-free by scoring saved eval reports with a Claude Code subagent, instead of only the slow z.ai glm-5.2 judge.

### Context / background
Honest measurement is a North-Star pillar ("measures itself on real PRs and reports real recall/precision"). Today the judge is `scripts/judge.ts` → a z.ai **glm-5.2** LLM-via-Pi call (~30–40s/call; a full before/after measurement's judging phase was ~40 min) that also **contends with the dogfood reviewer's shared z.ai quota**, and is **same-family** as most ranked candidates (self-preference risk — #49 AC2). A Claude Code subagent judge is: (a) fast (no z.ai latency), (b) free in-session, (c) genuinely **cross-family** for our GLM/deepseek reviewers. Validated informally this session: a subagent reproduced the grading contract in seconds. NOT a replacement for `scripts/judge.ts` in CI/cron (a Claude session isn't available there) — the two are complementary.

### Acceptance criteria
- **AC1:** a reusable judge-subagent protocol (a documented prompt + the report/manifest inputs) that applies the SAME strict grading contract as `src/eval/judge.ts` (per-defect: same root cause + location, not just same file) and returns per-case matched/total.
- **AC2:** agreement check — on ≥2 saved reports, the subagent judge's defect count is within the glm-5.2 judge's re-score range (they don't diverge systematically). Because it's cross-family, this also serves #49 AC2's cross-check (alongside deepseek).
- **AC3:** a short `docs/` (or ops-page) note: use the subagent judge for **interactive** measurement (fast/free/cross-family); use `scripts/judge.ts` (glm-5.2 default, or deepseek cross-family) for **programmatic/CI/offline**. When they disagree, prefer re-scoring both and reporting the spread (judge stochasticity is real — #49 AC1).

### Evidence / checks
Spawn the judge subagent on ≥2 saved `eval/reports/*.json`; compare its counts to `bun run scripts/judge.ts --report <same> --judge-repeats 3`. Record the comparison. No code required to pass AC1/AC2 (it's a protocol + doc); a thin helper is optional.

### Non-goals
Not replacing `scripts/judge.ts` (CI/cron need a headless judge). Not a paid Anthropic-API judge in the CLI (that's a separate option). Not changing the reviewer, only the measurement path.

### Expected artifact
A doc/protocol note (and this session's validation data), optionally a small helper. Mostly docs.

### Stop condition
None expected — measurement tooling, free, no trust-boundary or product-API surface. If the subagent judge systematically disagrees with the GLM judge (not just noise), surface that as a finding rather than silently preferring one.
issue.title: Fast cross-family judge via a Claude subagent (interactive measurement)

pr.body:
## What
Records the protocol + validation for judging saved eval reports with a **Claude Code subagent** instead of only the slow z.ai glm-5.2 judge (your suggestion). Docs-only: `docs/reference/subagent-judge.md`.

## Why (North Star: honest + fast measurement)
The glm-5.2 judge is ~30–40s/call (~40 min per 3-report set), **contends with the dogfood reviewer's shared z.ai quota**, and is same-family (self-preference risk). A subagent judge is ~1–4 min/report, free in-session, and cross-family.

## Validation (this session, 2 reports — AC2)
Subagent judge vs `scripts/judge.ts`, graded independently:
| report | subagent | scripts/judge.ts |
|---|---|---|
| glm-5.2 …12-17… | 4/11 | glm-5.2: 4–5 (median 4) |
| glm-5.2 …00-47… | 6/11 | glm-5.2: 6 · deepseek: 6 |
Agreed on both, on *different* expected numbers.

## Scope
Docs only; the doc is explicit that `scripts/judge.ts` stays the CI/programmatic path (a subagent needs a Claude session). Closes #61.

## What I need from the maintainer
No decision beyond merge/block.
pr.title: docs: fast cross-family defect judge via a Claude subagent (#61)
